symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral dependent acrocyanosis/calf ache on standing, resolving on elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral dependent cyanosis resolving instantly on elevation is classic venous pooling.
Bilateral, symmetric, elevation-resolving cyanosis is not the DVT pattern.
No single fixed pale/cold/pulseless limb argues strongly against arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fixed unilateral calf swelling/tenderness; no immobility/travel/surgery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent unilateral fixed calf features and DVT risk factors make DVT very unlikely, though legs can't be examined remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No true syncope or chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Near-syncope without true LOC/chest pain is less concerning, but ECG is still needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: High fall risk; cannot stand to prepare food, isolated

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cerebral hypoperfusion on standing while alone risks falls and poor intake.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural HR 72->135 with systolic drop; bilateral acrocyanosis resolving on elevation; soft calves; equal pulses; Homan negative (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic tachycardia with dependent cyanosis resolving on elevation and normal pulses is the neuropathic POTS pattern.
Soft non-tender calves with equal pulses and elevation resolution argue against DVT.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral dependent acrocyanosis and near-syncope on standing that resolve instantly on elevation, with no single ischaemic/DVT leg, is neuropathic POTS venous pooling — but legs/pulses and ECG can't be assessed remotely, so urgent in-person review plus fall-safety and supportive compression/salt-fluids are required, with 000 for a single pale/cold/pulseless leg, true syncope or chest pain.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person leg examination and pulse assessment (± Doppler/USS if any concern)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude DVT/limb ischaemia; cannot be done via chat.
To exclude arrhythmia and document the postural response.
To support management and exclude contributors.
